review_text: After my spectacular trip at Long Peso Village, Setulang and Tana Olen are equally awesome adventures. Love the places, the people and the experiences. Amazing! Speechless! review_text: Unique environment at the end of the road. Host families are happy to give you room and board, food is taken from jungle gardens on daily basis, this village is trying hard to protect their Dayak Kenyah heritage and their forest. No phone or Internet service without climbing the hill - but thats a good thing. We stayed with Pk Filius RT4. Come and extract yourself for a break from the world.
author: Anthony Vovers (Tony)

review_text:
Desa wisata yg menyajikan tempat indah.

Rumah adat yg berdiri megah bersanding indah dengan kehidupan masyarakat adat Dayak Lunda yeh yg sederhana namun memiliki kekhasan.

Fasilitas wilayah ini terdapat penginapan yang dapat dimanfaatkan untuk menginap, atau bisa juga menginap d rumah warga untuk lebih merasakan kehidupan masyarakat nya.

review_text: Very nice Dayak's tribe village with a big traditional aula calls Lamin or Betang House for any kind of ceremony. It's only take about an hour by car from the town of Malinau. They are offering traditional dancing, but I think you should ask for it first. Some villagers also offer Dayak's souvenirs such as bracelets, necklaces, hats, etc.
